mnemonics.title: DEMAT for Dematerialization
mnemonics.content:
D - Digitalize processes
E - Eliminate physical materials
M - Minimize environmental impact
A - Assess lifecycle impacts
T - Transform business models

acronyms.usage: Policy making producers responsible for product end-of-life management, encouraging dematerialization in design
Methodology to evaluate environmental impacts from cradle to grave
Delivering software online rather than physical installation, example of dematerialization
Connected devices enabling smart resource management and dematerialization of monitoring
Framework for sustainability: People, Planet, Profit

visualTips.visualization: Draw a linear arrow showing: Raw Materials → Manufacturing → Distribution → Use → Disposal. Show dematerialization arrows pointing inward at each stage to represent material reduction.
Use a scale: Strong = 100% elimination (digital), Weak = 50% reduction (lighter products). Show streaming on one end, lightweight phones on the other.
Draw a seesaw: efficiency gains on one side, increased consumption on the other. Show how they balance, reducing net benefits.
Before/After comparison: Paper documents stack vs cloud icon, CD stack vs streaming icon, physical store vs e-commerce cart.

analogies.analogy: Just as a person reduces food intake while maintaining energy levels, dematerialization reduces material use while maintaining functional value.
Raw materials flow like a river through manufacturing (rapids), distribution (downstream), use (lake), and disposal (delta). Dematerialization reduces the flow at each point.
Physical products are like solid objects casting shadows. Virtual products are like pure shadows - real in function but without physical substance.
Efficiency stretches the elastic band (reduces impact), but consumption snaps it back (increases use), limiting how much ground is covered.
